export_adapters.failure_behavior: Send failures are bounded, counted, and do not abort server startup or shutdown.
export_adapters.help_text: Export metrics to StatsD or DogStatsD using host:port, statsd://host:port, or dogstatsd://host:port
export_adapters.startup_behavior: Exporter starts after server startup and performs an immediate best-effort flush.
export_adapters.wire_format: StatsD line protocol over UDP; DogStatsD compatibility is the same line format without tags.

export_adapters.failure_behavior: POST failures are bounded, counted, and preserve buffered spans for retry on the next cycle.
export_adapters.help_text: Export metrics and spans to the package-owned OTLP-style HTTP collector endpoint
export_adapters.startup_behavior: Exporter starts after server startup and emits metrics/spans in periodic batches.
export_adapters.wire_format: Package-owned OTLP-style JSON envelope over HTTP POST.

notes.aggregation: Counters are monotonic totals; gauges report current in-process state.
notes.http3_scope: HTTP/3 metrics reflect the package-owned QUIC/HTTP/3 runtime only; they do not claim cross-vendor collector compatibility beyond the declared exporter adapters.
notes.stability: Metric names are package-owned public operator surface claims and are generated into docs/conformance/metrics_schema.*.

redaction_rules.connection_ids: dcid/scid values are redacted in emitted packet summaries
redaction_rules.network_endpoints: remote endpoint addresses are redacted from qlog output
redaction_rules.payload_bytes: raw packet payload bytes are not copied into qlog output

versioning.upgrade_rule: schema_version changes when redaction fields, event envelopes, or emitted packet summary fields change incompatibly
